newsSince: Material: on June 16, 2026 Wiley reported its FY26 Q4/full-year - a self-described 'breakout year.' Record profitability (adj EBITDA margin 26.2% all-time high, adj EPS +15% to $4.19, FCF +55% to $195M), AI revenue +23% to $49M with recurring AI inflecting $1M->$8M and a FY27 AI guide of $50M+, plus new IQVIA/OpenEvidence partnerships and a completed Emerald Publishing acquisition. Stock rose ~1.25% on print.

sources.label: Wiley Q4 FY26 earnings transcript (Motley Fool)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent-AI-node lens directly validated: the buried scientific-corpus AI-licensing line is now visible and recurring (AI rev +23% to $49M, recurring $1M->$8M, FY27 $50M+ guide) plus IQVIA/OpenEvidence/Anthropic deals and record FCF. Under-covered thesis confirmed by hard numbers; rank up.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Recurring AI licensing inflection + new lab/healthcare partnerships and FY27 $50M+ AI guide
verdicts.primaryRisk: Core adj revenue flat ex-AI; AI licensing terms could compress as content commoditizes
verdicts.bull: One of three scale owners of rights-cleared peer-reviewed science; AI line now recurring with record margins/FCF and a clear FY27 ramp.
verdicts.neutral: AI line still small vs total; legacy publishing flat. Re-rating partly priced after +52% YTD.
verdicts.bear: AI revenue is lumpy/license-driven; frontier labs may push pricing or build around licensed corpora.
